issue_body:
on executing my code I am facing this error
`./sample2D: symbol lookup error: /usr/local/lib/libftgl.so.2: undefined symbol: glad_glGenTextures`

```
all: sample2D

sample2D: Sample_GL3_2D.cpp glad.c
        g++ -o sample2D Sample_GL3_2D.cpp glad.c -lGL -lglfw -lGLU -lftgl -ldl -std=c++11

clean:
        rm sample2D

```

this is my Makefile

Some functions in the C output, that have no arguments, are generated with `()` instead of `(void)`. The syntax `()` means that the function takes an arbitrary number of arguments, while the `(void)` means that the function doesn't take any argument.

The syntax `()` will generate warnings in GCC/MinGW when the warning flag `-Wstrict-prototypes` is activated. Example:
```
./glad.h:1078:1: warning: function declaration isn’t a prototype [-Wstrict-prototypes]
 typedef void (APIENTRYP PFNGLFINISHPROC)();
 ^~~~~~~
```

This goes for the OpenGL functions and the function `close_gl()` in `glad.c`.

issue_title: Question/Discussion: Performance of subtraction of pint is slower than the naive approach
issue_body:
I am profiling my code (which uses pint) and I noticed that some array operations were surprisingly slow (like array subtraction with different units) compared to manual unit scaling. Below is an example that demonstrates this.

Question: Why is the pint approach slower? Is there something about pint which I missed which might speed up the process?

```python
class test_pint:
    def __init__(self, a, b):
        self.a = a
        self.b = b

    def test(self):
        self.a -= self.b

        return self.a

class test_pint_manual:
    def __init__(self, a, b):
        self.a = a
        self.b = b

    def test(self):
        units_b = Quantity(1., self.b.units)
        scale = units_b.to(self.a.units).magnitude
        _arr = self.b.magnitude
        _arr *= scale
        _arr2 = self.a.magnitude
        _arr2 -= _arr

        return self.a
```

Testing like this:

issue_title: Calling str() on an ip datagram changes its' length if capture has a non-max snaplen
issue_body:
```
>>> for tx,pkt in enumerate(pcap):
...   eth = dpkt.ethernet.Ethernet(pkt)
...   if eth.type == dpkt.ethernet.ETH_TYPE_IP:
...     ipd = eth.data
...     prel = ipd.len
...     s = str(ipd)
...     postl = ipd.len
...     print "%d - %d" % (prel, postl)

48 - 48
544 - 186
32 - 32

544 - 186
32 - 32
544 - 186
32 - 32
544 - 186
32 - 32
544 - 186
32 - 32
544 - 186
32 - 32
544 - 186
32 - 32
```

`__str__` in `dpkt.ip` sets `self.len` to `self.__len__()`, which means its value changes on packets that are cut off, because `__len__` uses the actual buffer length, and not the total length field.  This is almost certainly what `len()` should do, but the `ip.len` field should never get overwritten with "new" data.

issue_title: Add special handling for TANGENT_BOTH intersections.
issue_labels: [{'id': 518253247, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dhermes/bezier/labels/language:%20fortran', 'name': 'language: fortran', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 693077457, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dhermes/bezier/labels/language:%20python', 'name': 'language: python', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 476633368, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dhermes/bezier/labels/type:%20enhancement', 'name': 'type: enhancement', 'color': '84b6eb', 'default': False}, {'id': 476633371, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dhermes/bezier/labels/type:%20question', 'name': 'type: question', 'color': 'cc317c', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
From https://github.com/dhermes/bezier/commit/b89b2b1de1726cdc9f508bd761f4c20e7d655321:

> This is a "robustness" change and (for now) it allows a "degenerate" curved polygon of intersection. What will likely happen is that the change to `surface_intersections.json` will be reverted and code will be added to split the intersection at the `TANGENT_BOTH` point.

As of right now, a `TANGENT_BOTH` intersection leads to a single "degenerate" curved polygon where two of the sides actually collapse to meet at a single point before opening (see [15Q-16Q][1] and [10Q-40Q][2]):

![surfaces15q_and_16q](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520669/36171797-d1874086-10b8-11e8-856e-d5a0c5867ea4.png)

![surfaces10q_and_40q](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520669/36171802-d557269a-10b8-11e8-86ec-a12c814725f5.png)

However, it would be equally correct (or maybe more correct) to split those curved polygons at that point of tangency (see [`classify_intersection5.png`][3] and [`classify_intersection9.png`][4]):

![classify_intersection5](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520669/36171786-c7864e2e-10b8-11e8-900a-6d2c76523c79.png)

![classify_intersection9](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520669/36171782-c3c87aaa-10b8-11e8-973c-88490ba820aa.png)

[1]: https://github.com/dhermes/bezier/blob/4b773f340145aceaf7f8764411e13aadd7eb83b8/docs/images/surfaces15Q_and_16Q.png
[2]: https://github.com/dhermes/bezier/blob/4b773f340145aceaf7f8764411e13aadd7eb83b8/docs/images/surfaces10Q_and_40Q.png
[3]: https://github.com/dhermes/bezier/blob/4b773f340145aceaf7f8764411e13aadd7eb83b8/docs/images/classify_intersection5.png
[4]: https://github.com/dhermes/bezier/blob/4b773f340145aceaf7f8764411e13aadd7eb83b8/docs/images/classify_intersection9.png

issue_title: Why validate that 'iat' is not in the future?
issue_body:
In https://github.com/jpadilla/pyjwt/blob/c5ee34e86bc42bef60ef6e701df569c2c86a5d5d/jwt/api_jwt.py#L129:

``` python
if iat > (now + leeway):
    raise InvalidIssuedAtError('Issued At claim (iat) cannot be in'
                               ' the future.')
```

I just debugged an issue in prod where `jwt.decode()` failed because of this. Mostly because the other party's jwt lib added 'iat' a few seconds or minutes ahead of our clock time ('clock skew' as mentioned in JWT specs).

I can't find any place in the specs that says that a JWT should be invalid if 'iat' is in the future. It seems like it's just there to be informative. I can use 'nbf' if I want to specify a ["time before which the token MUST NOT be accepted for processing"](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-ietf-oauth-jwt-bearer-12#section-3)

I consulted
- https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7519
- https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-ietf-oauth-jwt-bearer-12

So either
1. I'm wrong and there is a JWT spec that says this is important to check. I want to know this, because if it's out there, I shouldn't just catch these errors from PyJWT and `pass`. Regardless of whether @jpadilla wants to remove that `raise` in his lib.
2. PyJWT is checking that unnecessarily, and we should remove it to be more compliant

As I was scanning over the setup.py I noticed the following
```py
    license             = "BSD",

        'License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License',
```

issue_title: fix point order when converting higher-order elements from gmsh to xdmf
use random.SystemRandom() or os.urandom for secret generation
issue_body:
uh yeah, using a pseudorandom number generator (PRNG) for secrets (passwords) is not acceptable, if your going to keep this published on pypi, I must strongly encourage the heedence of the documented prng module https://docs.python.org/2/library/random.html

In that random.SystemRandom or os.urandom be used to choose the random value.

> Warning
> 
> The pseudo-random generators of this module should not be used for security purposes. Use os.urandom() or SystemRandom if you require a cryptographically secure pseudo-random number generator. 

The premise of PRNG is that the results are seeded and entirely reproducible, a trivial replay attack can produce the passwords generated here.

issue_title: ram DB is very slow on finalizing an otherwise small calibration problem.
issue_body:
While dealing with a use case where SCE-UA calibrates an external model, structurally not very different from the [hymod examples](https://github.com/thouska/spotpy/blob/master/spotpy/examples/spot_setup_hymod_python.py) but a simpler model, I found the runtime rather slow, and the end of the sampling getting stuck for minutes before finally returning. 

After debugging it seems that the ram DB is storing the data points of each simulation time series resulting. This is compounded by  numerous `np.transpose` in the `finalize` method of the class `ram`.

```python
    def save(self, objectivefunction, parameterlist, simulations=None,
             chains=1):
        self.ram.append(self.dim_dict['like'](objectivefunction) +
                        self.dim_dict['par'](parameterlist) +
                        self.dim_dict['simulation'](simulations) +
                        [chains])
```

if `simulations` is indeed expected to be an "array" (time series) in the case of hydrologic models, I am puzzled they would find their way by default into a calibration log.

Happy to contribute something, but I need clarifications as to the intentions of the design for the log databases.

issue_title: Some types of messages can not be deleted, even by the author
issue_body:
In some cases, `message.author.id == client.user.id` but `client.delete_message(message)` will error. This is because some types of messages can not be deleted by the author.

The types of messages that I have so far observed to have this behavior can only be created in a private message between two user accounts. Namely, the messages that occur when you start a voice call.

Feel free to close this issue, as I've never observed this issue with actual bot accounts. However, I do believe it should be noted in the documentation that being the author of a message does not necessarily mean you can delete it.

issue_title: Replace req, resp, meta, params, kwargs, etc. with more general context parameter that can store all of this information
issue_body:
This is an enchancement that will surely break compatibility but it will be a solution to problem with deciding which method should have access to which of this basic data structures.

Because there is always single resource instance on given route no state can be stored in this object between method calls. This means that all required data must be always passed to each method that requires it. Because of that `graceful` needs to make very opinionated decision about what is available on each step of processing the request. Although this helps developer to focus on what is important in specific method handler (e.g., retrieve, list, create in generic views) it is very hard to extend if something non usual needs to be done - often it requires overriding of many methods.

We could also consider if this context could be passed to serialisers so more complex things could be done like: field serialisation that depends on query string parameters.

issue_title: FUTURE: parallelize training up our three different prediction_interval models
train up subpredictors in parallel using parallel map

issue_body:
right now it's quite memory inefficient- we essentially end up holding the entire thing in memory twice. 

and it's somewhat computationally expensive (not hugely so, but noticeable). and it's basically doing the same thing each time. 

so rather than doing this 8 times in parallel (essentially holding 16x our data in memory), run it once, before we dispatch data. 

this will also give us more incentive to parallelize DataFrameVectorizer, since it will now be a single-threaded blocking operation, rather than nested inside an already-parallelized operation. 

The issues we run into are particularly around subpredictors. For each subpredictor, there has to be some logic for which columns to keep, and which to ignore. And this is going to be different for each subpredictor. 

However, I think we can handle that even after vectorization. 

this also means we'll have to refactor how we do feature selection. 

effectively, we'll have to build out our own feature selection module that sits after DFVectorizer. 

our custom feature selection module will have to:
1. ignore any columns in the vectorized data that we should not know about (which i think will just be all the subpredictor y val columns). 
2. ignore any values that have not been chosen by feature selection for that particular subpredictor. 

to accomplish #1, it the feature selection module must have knowledge of the column_descriptions object, and the dfv.vocabulary_ dict. From there it's some pretty straightforward logic. 

2 is just re-implementing scikit-learn's feature selection logic. 

This also has all kinds of effects on the rest of the pipeline. 

for example, when we get feature names for analytics, we must get them from the new feature selection module. 

we will also have to not restrict DataFrameVectorizer, like we currently are. instead, we'll leave the logic for removing vals in our new custom feature selection module. 

this means we can remove the somewhat hacky duplicate code in _construct_pipeline, which will be much cleaner. 

We may have to do something differently in our column naming around subpredictors. we might be ok, but it's worth looking into.

issue_title: No execution on order_target_value and order_target_percent happens if open price of the bar is different from close price of the previous bar
issue_body: Currently size of the position for `order_target_value` and `order_target_percent` is calculated based on the close of the bar at which order is issued. By default this order is executed at the open of the next bar. If this open is higher (for long signals) than previous close, then position can be skipped totally due to lack of funds. Such behavior was observed with targets of 95-100% of the broker value.

To Electrum clients, when queried with `server.peers.subscribe`, an example reply is:
```
[elec.luggs.co] <-- {'result': [['luggscoqbymhvnkp.onion', 'luggscoqbymhvnkp.onion', ['v1.1', 't80']], ['127.0.0.1', 's2.noip.pl', ['v1.1', 's50102']], ['bauerjhejlv6di7s.onion', 'bauerjhejlv6di7s.onion', ['v1.1', 's50002', 't50001']], ['4zwgpbfa6hnpse4m.onion', '4zwgpbfa6hnpse4m.onion', ['v1.1', 's50101']], ['ozahtqwp25chjdjd.onion', 'ozahtqwp25chjdjd.onion', ['v1.1', 's50002', 't50001']], ['gttvzmfh23pxqiw4.onion', 'gttvzmfh23pxqiw4.onion', ['v1.1', 's50002', 't50001']], ['gyn2vguc35viks2b.onion', 'gyn2vguc35viks2b.onion', ['v1.1', 't50001']], ['bauerjsb3k4orv2h.onion', 'bauerjsb3k4orv2h.onion', ['v1.1', 's50002', 't50001']], ['j5jfrdthqt5g25xz.onion', 'j5jfrdthqt5g25xz.onion', ['v1.0', 's50002', 't50001']], ['127.0.0.1', 'electrum.antumbra.se', ['v1.1', 's50002', 't50001']], ['127.0.0.1', 'elec.luggs.co', ['v1.1', 's443']], ['qtornadoklbgdyww.onion', 'qtornadoklbgdyww.onion', ['v1.1', 's50002', 't50001']], ['o6nvntbo3qsn36dm.onion', 'o6nvntbo3qsn36dm.onion', ['v1.0', 's50002', 't50001']]], 'jsonrpc': '2.0', 'id': 3}
```

I'm guessing this reply is short as it's filtered to unique IPv4 /16 subnets, as in
https://github.com/kyuupichan/electrumx/blob/305fd897406e6f6609a033a7d633db4729b1b8e6/docs/PEER_DISCOVERY.rst#response-to-serverpeerssubscribe-rpc-call

> In ElectrumX, "recently" is taken to be the last 24 hours. Only one peer from each IPv4/16 netmask is returned, and the number of onion peers is limited.

Clearly, all "127.0.0.1" peers share a /16. :)

To sum up, it seems e-x saves all server peers with the Tor proxy's IP.

I am getting this error when calling server.features
```
ERROR:ElectrumX:[36] Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/electrumx-1.2-py3.6.egg/lib/jsonrpc.py", line 528, in process_single_request

    return handler(**kw_args)

    return self.controller.peer_mgr.my_clearnet_peer().features

```

Hi 

   I love the fact that Host local facts get added to  the fancy html. How can a get them also added to sql or csv templates?   For some reason the setup module  does not list vm  on a  hypervisor so I added local host facts.

ansible -m setup --tree 10.133.12.0/ 10.133.12.0-200

2.  Create the fancy html that has the data: 

ansible-cmdb 10.133.12.0/  > /var/www/html/inventory/10.131.12.0-200.html


 Steps SQL: 

1. Create SQL data: 

ansible-cmdb -t sql -i 10.133.12.0-200 out > cmdb.sql

2. Look at sql data: 

  mysql -uroot -p ansiblecmdb < cmdb.sql 

The Host local facts are not in the database?

=================================================

Steps to create local facts on remote KVM server:
    
shell commands:
mkdir /etc/ansible/facts.d
mkdir -p /etc/ansible/facts.d
cp virtlst.py /etc/ansible/facts.d/virtlst.fact 
chmod 755  /etc/ansible/facts.d/virtlst.fact 
   

Script:

# cat virtlst.py 
=========================================
#!/usr/bin/python

from subprocess import *
import  json

b = Popen(["virsh", "list", "--all"], stdout=PIPE)

out = b.stdout.read()

each_virt = []
out_lines = out.strip().split("\n")[2:-1]
for lines in out_lines:
     v = lines.split()[1]
     each_virt.append(v) 
my_dict = { 'virts': each_virt }
print json.dumps(my_dict)

================================================


Run 
# ./virtlst.py 
================================================
{"virts": ["va-openstackallinone-01-ops-vm-tr", "va-zenoss-01-ops-vm-tr-clone", "va-zabbix-01-ops-vm-tr"]}

------------------------------------

It looks like ansible_cmdb tries to parse every file recursively in the given inventory folder as a host file.
E.g, with the following structure
```
inventories/
  - hosts
  - group_vars/
    - all.yml
    - webservers.yml
  - company_name_dev/
    - hosts
    - group_vars/
      - all.yml
      - database.yml
```

`group_vars/all.yml`:
```
tags:
  - test
  - dev
```
and the command `ansible-cmdb -d -i ~/inventories/ ~/out/ > overview.html`, the `hosts` files are parsed correctly (they give the correct groups to hosts), but `group_vars/all.yml` is parsed as a host file as well.
It creates a host named `tags`, and then fails to parse the following lines (`Unsupported vars syntax. Skipping line: - test`).

After the host parsing step, the `group_vars` folders are searched, but only at the root level. `company_name_dev/group_vars/all.yml` isn't read.

Would it be possible to skip parsing `group_vars` (and `host_vars` probably?) as hosts files, and look for `group_vars` folders anywhere in the inventory tree?

issue_title: support for wifi370 led strip controllers
issue_body:
I have an (older?) wifi led strip controller, described as wifi370 on the box. 
https://nl.aliexpress.com/item/Original-Wifi-RGB-LED-controller-for-Iphone-Ipad-Android-mobile-phone-2-3Version-IOS-system-DC7/1032823060.html

this device is supported by the magic home app, and it seems to be a 'propper'  flux_led device, but it has some significant differences. 

this library recognizes it while scanning, but this device uses a different set of commands and does not use a checksum to send data.
eg, turn on is  CC 23 33 rather than 71 23 0F
status is EF 01 77 rather than 81 8A 8B

this device also returns only 11 bytes of status rather than the 14 this library expects.

I managed to do a python conversion based on https://github.com/sidoh/ledenet_api

it can be found here: https://github.com/marcwagner/alarmclock/blob/master/ledenet_api.py

I can probably integrate my code into this project so that it can also work with homeassistant. However, due to some of the differences I am worried about breaking compatibility. 

so, two questions: should I integrate my code and is there an easy way to recognize the different flavors of protocol?

issue_body:
A string passed to `get_display` in the format of `dd month yyyy - dd month yyyy` will yield different ordering if the day is a single digit.

---

**OSX 10.11.6**
**Python 3.5.1**
**python-bidi 0.4.0**

---

Steps to reproduce:

```
get_display('٢٦ أكتوبر ١٩٤٥-١٥ أبريل ١٩٤٦')
'١٩٤٦ ليربأ ١٥-١٩٤٥ ربوتكأ ٢٦'
get_display('٢ أكتوبر ١٩٤٥-١٥ أبريل ١٩٤٦')
'١٩٤٦ ليربأ ١٥-٢ أكتوبر ١٩٤٥'
```

Read from right to left, the above will generate:

issue_title: Incorrect behavior when reading merged cells
issue_body:
When reading in merged cells, only the first cell in the merged range gets the correct value. This can be especially frustrating when using multi-level headings since they won't import correctly into the DataFrame.

Digging into this, it looks like it's an issue with the older Sheets APIs. This seems to have been fixed in v4, but `gspread` uses v3. I might look into adding a workaround using v4 directly in `gspread_pandas`.

issue_title: Feature request: Show current wall time instead of elapsed time
issue_body:
#### Description

For long running jobs where individual tasks may take a long time or stall, `Elapsed Time` becomes hard to interpret. What would be more useful for me is a `Current Time` indicator. This would allow me, at a glance, to see if the jobs are still completing, or if they have stalled and haven't made progress in hours.

#### Code

One possible interface:
```py
for job in ProgressBar()(create_jobs(), current_time=True):
   ...
```

which would output:
```
...
  1% (177 of 10001) |              | Current Time: 16:39:23 ETA: 1 day, 12:25:58
  1% (178 of 10001) |              | Current Time: 16:39:23 ETA: 1 day, 12:13:50
  1% (179 of 10001) |              | Current Time: 16:39:49 ETA: 1 day, 12:25:10

issue_title: Support multiple json files for Swagger 2.0
issue_body:
Currently, we only support Swagger 2.0 specs that are wholly contained in a single ``swagger.json`` file.  As part of [bravado-core's refactoring to support recursive $refs] (https://github.com/Yelp/bravado-core/issues/35), serving up a swagger.json that references other json files also becomes possible. As such, clients will create http requests for other files that embedded $refs point to.

To support this feature, pyramid-swagger needs to be able to serve up all files that make up a spec and register the appropriate views.  See [register_api_doc_endpoints] (https://github.com/striglia/pyramid_swagger/blob/ae2de7770d2eced733490adbdf51cf1d895f92af/pyramid_swagger/__init__.py#L46)

# problem

I would like to have multiple settings files to use according to the environment 'test_settings', 'prod_settings', 'amazon_settings', 'openshift_settings' etc... but I would like the ability to mix then

Currently I can use the worst solution ever

```python
try:
    from local_settings import *
except ImportError:
    pass
```

But, it is usual to avoid using "imports" inside settings file, that kind of file should be a simple repository of **KEY:value**, besides that, there is the fact that the hierarchy order can change between environments.

# solution

So in order to do have this I propose this **SSEP** (Simple Settings Enhancement Proposal) LOL

having 3 settings files in project 

    settings.py
    test_settings.cfg
    vendor/amazon_settings.py

```bash
python myproject.py --settings=settings,test_settings.cfg,vendor/amazon_settings.py
```

1. can accept only the name of settings module, without the need to specify the file extensions
2. Settings can reside in **.py** or in **.cfg** files
3. can accept full directory path
4. Load the settings in the specified order, overriding the conflicted keys (dict.update automatically does that)

# caveats

Maybe it is not simple settings anymore! LOL

issue_title: DynamicDocumentSerializer should ignore values passed for read only fields instead of raising an exception
issue_body:
Hello,

when I pass to a DynamicDocumentSerializer some data that applies to a read-only field (could be "id", but I think it applies to others too), it raises an AssertionError.

DocumentSerializer (and I think DRF) would silently ignore this value, but DynamicDocumentSerializer throws an error on that. There is a comment in the code (https://github.com/umutbozkurt/django-rest-framework-mongoengine/blob/master/rest_framework_mongoengine/serializers.py#L629) explaining it, but I find this behaviour a bit annoying in practice. It basically means I have to strip the id from the object manually before passing it to the serializer. Is there some way around it? Or can the code be changed so that values for read_only fields are just ignored?



Some example code that triggers the issue:
```python
class TestDocument(DynamicDocument):
    pass

class TestDocumentSerializer(DynamicDocumentSerializer):
    class Meta:
        model = TestDocument
        fields = '__all__'

s = TestDocumentSerializer(data={})
s.is_valid()
testDoc = s.save()

s_data = TestDocumentSerializer(testDoc).data

s2 = TestDocumentSerializer(testDoc, data=s_data)
s2.is_valid()
```

https://developers.facebook.com/docs/marketing-api/advideo/v2.9

However, the parameters to create an ad video is different between the 2 calls - 

the second approach, to create AdVideo while setting the parent_id to the ad account accepts filepath, while the other method of creating AdVideo as part of the an AdAccount accepts file_url.

FB SDK deprecated the second option (of creating a directly AdVideo), however, I don't find an equivalent method in AdAccount -> create ad video that allows uploading from file path.

See AdVideo:

https://github.com/facebook/facebook-python-ads-sdk/blob/master/facebookads/adobjects/advideo.py

See the way to create it via AdAccount:
https://github.com/facebook/facebook-python-ads-sdk/blob/master/facebookads/adobjects/adaccount.py

issue_title: Naive shim around $.data breaks much code
issue_body:
The shim around .data doesn't support the same set of overloads as the core function. It assumes the second parameter is the `name` property, whereas it can be an object to set a bunch of properties simultaneously.

See: https://github.com/jquery/jquery-migrate/blob/master/src/data.js#L7
vs: https://github.com/jquery/jquery/blob/master/src/data.js

Among other things, this breaks AngularJS.

issue_title: # Wrong Order of Arguments in the Code Examples
issue_body:
# Wrong Order of Arguments in the Code Examples

I followed the steps provided in README.md to install `deepspeech` via pip, get the pretrained model and test it on an audio file.

I initiated my virtual environment via `pipenv`

```sh
pipenv install --three
wget -O - https://github.com/mozilla/DeepSpeech/releases/download/v0.1.1/deepspeech-0.1.1-models.tar.gz | tar xvfz -
pipenv shell
pip3 install deepspeech
deepspeech models/output_graph.pb models/alphabet.txt models/lm.binary models/trie audio/file_3.wav
```

It seems that the **order** of the arguments in the README.md is **wrong** 

In the first paragraph and in the section `Using the Python Package/Installing DeepSpeech Python binding`:

```
deepspeech models/output_graph.pb models/alphabet.txt my_audio_file.wav
```

```
deepspeech models/output_graph.pbmm models/alphabet.txt models/lm.binary models/trie my_audio_file.wav
```

The correct order (as shown in the gif in README.md) seems to be:

```
deepspeech models/output_graph.pb audio/my_audio_file.wav models/alphabet.txt models/lm.binary models/trie 
```

Thanks for updating it or correcting me :).  And thanks for the awesome project!

issue_title: if LOGOUT_REDIRECT_URL is not set, it defaults to None
issue_body:
If `LOGOUT_REDIRECT_URL` is not set, then it defaults to `None` in Django 1.10+.

The settings docs suggest it defaults to `/`, but it doesn't. So if you don't set it, then after going through the `OIDCLogoutView` dispatch code, the user gets redirected to `None` which ends up being `/oidc/logout/None` which is a 404.

This issue covers figuring this out.

issue_title: Change variable name `mozilla_django_oidc.middleware.RefreshIDToken` to something else
issue_body:
Kind of a nitpick, however, `RefreshIDToken` is a confusing name because refresh tokens are a very specific thing: non-expiring access tokens.
In this case, this is the opposite, its a token that expires and needs periodic refresh.

issue_title: Seperate out AWS into a seperate repo
issue_body:
In this issue I propose:

* We copy out the code for aws-related stuff into it's own repository. I created a an empty repository, see [luigi-orchestrator/luigi-aws#1](https://github.com/luigi-orchestrator/luigi-aws/issues/1). Add to pypi
* We remove the implementation from here and replace it with a package dependency. We keep the tests as a way to check backward compatibility
* Celebrate. We have accomplished:
    * First (or second) attempt of splitting the kitchen-sink contrib/ folder.
    * It becomes so much easier to work with the repos if they are separate.

This is also a bit in the direction of #1897, but much more trivial, it requires no extra software. Just a build dependency.

Note that this design consciously suggests different repos, as we want different travis builds, different people being pinged on changes, different set of owners. etc. etc.

issue_title: Non-ASCII characters in archiveDescription causes upload to raise InvalidSignatureException
issue_body:
The [AWS Glacier doc states that](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonglacier/latest/dev/api-archive-post.html):

> When uploading an archive, you can optionally specify an archive description of up to 1,024 printable ASCII characters.

When a user provides non-ASCII chars, the upload fails with a misleading `InvalidSignatureException`. A specific check and a specific error would be more user friendly.

Example below:

```
response = client.upload_archive(
    vaultName=CONFIG_AWS_GLACIER['vault_name'],
    archiveDescription='é',
    body='abc',
)
```

```
ClientError: An error occurred (InvalidSignatureException) when calling the UploadArchive operation: The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided. Check your AWS Secret Access Key and signing method. Consult the service documentation for details.
```

issue_title: Sorting in TreeModifier.set_done() seems to be inconsistent with git's own sorting -> breaks repository
issue_body:
git enforced tree objects to be sorted by name. GitPython enables users to sort their tree's by calling TreeModifier.set_done(), see:
https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/blob/master/git/objects/tree.py#L45

This sorting somehow seems to be inconsistent with git's own sorting mechanism, which means adding a tree might break the git repository. This means "git fsck" will throw errors and some remotes (gitlab for example) will no accept the commit. Anyways this bug leases the git object store in an broken state which cannot be fixed easily. I had to delete multiple branches from projects I manage because of their broken state so far.

About the problem itself:

GitPython used core Python functions to sort the file list. This looks correct, but has one major difference to what git itself does. If you have two files beginning with the same chars (for example "file" and "file.second") their ordering will differ:

What git does:
1) "file.second"
2) "file"

What GitPython does:
1) "file"
2) "file.second"

We found this problem using fabdeploit for deployments using git, which allows us to add/removed files to/from the git repository. This way tree's will be modified which means sorting is essential.

Please fix this. ;-)

issue_title: LOCALE_PATHS is not settled up if I choose several languages in wizard
issue_body:
After installation with several languages, for example `en` and `se` I've got `settings.py` with several languages:
```
LANGUAGES = (
    ## Customize this
    ('en', gettext('en')),
    ('se', gettext('se')),
)
```

But without ``LOCALE_PATHS``. This variable is important for switching languages in middleware ``django.middleware.locale.LocaleMiddleware``. And after installation is impossible to use multi language website, because language is not switching by url ``http://mysite/se``. Such request redirected to ``http://mysite/en/se`` and user get an error ``404 Not Found``.

To fix it you need:
Add to settings.py:
```
LOCALE_PATHS = (
    os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'locale'),
)
```

Call for each language
```
./manage makemessages --locale se
```

Compile messages
```
./manage compilemessages --locale se
```


I can propose a PR for this bug, just say that you merge it.

issue_title: Extraneous virtualenv created when installing VCS dependencies using `--system`
issue_body:
Describe the issue briefly here.

I noticed an issue in my docker builds this morning using this `Dockerfile`:

```Dockerfile
FROM python:3.6

WORKDIR /app

COPY . .
COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /
RUN chmod +x /docker-entrypoint.sh
RUN pip install -U pip
RUN pip install pipenv
RUN pipenv install --deploy --system
RUN python -m pipenv.help
ENV PYTHONPATH /app
ENTRYPOINT ["/docker-entrypoint.sh"]
```

(Pretty standard stuff, I don't believe Docker is the issue here)

Here is a log of the docker build:

issue_title: Dependency resolution when package version conflicts python version
issue_body:
I have python 3.6 environment and using [us](https://pypi.org/project/us/) package
I'm using [us==1.0.0](https://pypi.org/project/us/1.0.0/):
* which supports python 3.6 (looks good so far)
* but depends on [jellyfish==0.5.6](https://pypi.org/project/jellyfish/0.5.6/) (not py3.6?)

`pipenv` installs [jellyfish==0.6.1](https://pypi.org/project/jellyfish/0.6.1/) which supports py3.6
So we end up with:
* jellyfish=0.6.1
* us=1.0.0 which requires 0.5.6

pipenv raises error about version conflicts

`Could not find a version that matches jellyfish==0.5.6,==0.6.1`

1. I'm wondering if dependency resolution works correctly?
2. If works ok what's the solution for such case?
3. Shouldn't be jellyfish==0.5.6 treated as py3.6 compatible?

issue_title: Support a target directory for vendoring (similar to pip --target)
issue_body:
We currently vendor some of our dependencies to a target directory, using `pip install -t`.  It would be great for pipenv to support this functionality!  This is our main blocker to further use.

I think right now it may be possible to accomplish by having pipenv generate a `requirements.txt` and pass it to pip to do the install, but that's very manual, and loses features like integrity-checking, so it would be much nicer for pipenv to do it.

(I mentioned this at PyCon, just wanted to make a github issue too so I can follow it.)

issue_title: auditwheel should retag linux by manylinux1 instead of adding a new tag
issue_body:
auditwheel has a command name `addtag` to rename a wheel file to insert the `manylinux1_x86_64` platform tag into its filename (in addition to the existing `linux_x86_64`) and similarly for `manylinux1_i686` / `linux_i686`.

This is an issue because uploading wheel files with the `linux_x86_64` / `linux_i686` platform tags to warehouse or PyPI is forbidden.

Instead `auditwheel repair` should probably replace the `linux` based tags by `manylinux1` tags whenever the repair operation succeeds.

issue_title: Cannot impor DLFCN.py on 64bit CentOS 6.7 with Python 2.7 SCL because of missing system plat-linux folder in sys.path
issue_body:
## Summary

On a CentOS 6.7 64 bit VM, the DLFCN module (needed by one library) cannot be imported in an activated virtual environment **unless** I explicitly set the `PYTHONPATH` env var to `/opt/rh/python27/root/usr/lib64/python2.7/plat-linux2`.

## Environment

* CentOS 6.7 64 bit (vagrant box `bento/centos-6.7`)
* Python 2.7.5 (from the SCL repo)
* virtualenv 14.0.6 (latest stable from PyPI to date)

The same **does not happen** on Ubuntu 14.04 LTS.

## Possible cause

I think that the root cause is that the system `plat-linux` folder (that contains `DLFCN.py`) is **not** added to `sys.path` on CentOS while it's added on Ubuntu:

### sys.path on CentOS 6.7

```bash
(dlfcn_test) [vagrant@app2 ~]$ python -c "import sys, pprint; pprint.pprint(sys.path)"
['',
 '/home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/lib64/python27.zip',
 '/home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/lib64/python2.7',
 '/home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/lib64/python2.7/plat-linux2',
 '/home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/lib64/python2.7/lib-tk',
 '/home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/lib64/python2.7/lib-old',
 '/home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/lib64/python2.7/lib-dynload',
 '/opt/rh/python27/root/usr/lib64/python2.7',
 '/opt/rh/python27/root/usr/lib/python2.7',
 '/home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/lib/python2.7/site-packages']
```

On CentOS with Python 2.7 SCL, the system `plat-linux`  is not in sys.path.


### sys.path on Ubuntu 14.04
```bash
(dlfcn_test) vagrant@app1:~$ python -c "import sys, pprint; pprint.pprint(sys.path)"
['',
 '/home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/lib/python2.7',
 '/home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/lib/python2.7/plat-x86_64-linux-gnu',
 '/home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/lib/python2.7/lib-tk',
 '/home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/lib/python2.7/lib-old',
 '/home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/lib/python2.7/lib-dynload',
 '/usr/lib/python2.7',
 '/usr/lib/python2.7/plat-x86_64-linux-gnu',
 '/usr/lib/python2.7/lib-tk',
 '/home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages',
 '/home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/lib/python2.7/site-packages']
```

On Ubuntu, sys.path contains `/usr/lib/python2.7/plat-x86_64-linux-gnu`

## How to reproduce

See interactive bash shell below

### CentOS 6.7 (broken)

```bash
[vagrant@app2 ~]$ cat /etc/centos-release
CentOS release 6.7 (Final)
[vagrant@app2 ~]$ env # output has been slightly edited
MANPATH=/opt/rh/python27/root/usr/share/man:
HOSTNAME=app2
TERM=xterm-256color
SHELL=/bin/bash
SSH_TTY=/dev/pts/0
USER=vagrant
LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/rh/python27/root/usr/lib64
PATH=/opt/rh/python27/root/usr/bin:/usr/local/bin:/bin:/usr/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/sbin:/sbin:/home/vagrant/bin
PWD=/home/vagrant
SHLVL=1
HOME=/home/vagrant
LOGNAME=vagrant
XDG_DATA_DIRS=/opt/rh/python27/root/usr/share
SSH_CONNECTION=10.0.2.2 46675 10.0.2.15 22
PKG_CONFIG_PATH=/opt/rh/python27/root/usr/lib64/pkgconfig
[vagrant@app2 ~]$ python --version
Python 2.7.5
[vagrant@app2 ~]$ virtualenv --version
14.0.6
[vagrant@app2 ~]$ virtualenv dlfcn_test
New python executable in /home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/bin/python
Installing setuptools, pip, wheel...done.
[vagrant@app2 ~]$ . /home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/bin/activate
(dlfcn_test) [vagrant@app2 ~]$ python -c "import DLFCN"
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
ImportError: No module named DLFCN
(dlfcn_test) [vagrant@app2 ~]$ deactivate
[vagrant@app2 ~]$ python -c "import DLFCN"
[vagrant@app2 ~]$ . /home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/bin/activate
(dlfcn_test) [vagrant@app2 ~]$ PYTHONPATH=/opt/rh/python27/root/usr/lib64/python2.7/plat-linux2 python -c "import DLFCN"
```

### Ubuntu 14.04 (works)

```bash
vagrant@app1:~$ cat /etc/lsb-release
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=14.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=trusty
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 14.04.3 LTS"
vagrant@app1:~$ env  # output has been slightly edited
TERM=xterm-256color
SHELL=/bin/bash
USER=vagrant
MAIL=/var/mail/vagrant
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/usr/games:/usr/local/games
PWD=/home/vagrant
SHLVL=1
HOME=/home/vagrant
LOGNAME=vagrant
vagrant@app1:~$ python --version
Python 2.7.6
vagrant@app1:~$ virtualenv --version
14.0.6
vagrant@app1:~$ virtualenv dlfcn_test
New python executable in /home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/bin/python2.7
Also creating executable in /home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/bin/python
Installing setuptools, pip, wheel...done.
vagrant@app1:~$ . /home/vagrant/dlfcn_test/bin/activate
(dlfcn_test) vagrant@app1:~$ python -c "import DLFCN"
```


Many thanks.

issue_body: Tick tupels, according to the documentation are supposed to reach only the bolts overriding process_tick() where thay will be processed. (copied from [developer forum post](https://community.tableau.com/thread/206900))

Validate that the document is correct (valid XML) and matches the known format of the Tableau document for that target product version, then validate that the document in terms of acceptable Tableau content (are the data connection class types valid, do the columns added to the rows/columns shelves actually present in the datasource, etc.). In other words, both syntactic and semantic validation.

issue_title: Not all imported utility functions are described in the docs
issue_labels: [{'id': 233604065, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwyMzM2MDQwNjU=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/scikit-image/scikit-image/labels/good%20first%20issue', 'name': 'good first issue', 'color': 'f4aead', 'default': True}, {'id': 298491501, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwyOTg0OTE1MDE=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/scikit-image/scikit-image/labels/type:%20documentation', 'name': 'type: documentation', 'color': 'fbda04', 'default': False}, {'id': 298491657, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwyOTg0OTE2NTc=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/scikit-image/scikit-image/labels/type:%20maintenance', 'name': 'type: maintenance', 'color': '3d8bbf', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/1151b511a10ec2568d72b94469afb09040a5ed9d/skimage/__init__.py#L46

Proposed fix:
  1. Only import the utilities functions that we describe.
  2. Describe all utility functions that are actually imported.

To do 1. you would need to specify the functions we import in the line
```python
from .util.dtypes import *
```

To do 2. you would need to look at the file `skimage.util.dtypes` at the line
```python
__all__ = [ ... ]
```
and give a short description of the functions that are imported.



Python lesson: the list [`__all__`](https://docs.python.org/3.6/tutorial/modules.html#importing-from-a-package) describes the functions that are imported when you use `from XXXX import *`

Currently the  [skimage.draw](http://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/api/skimage.draw.html) doesn't support drawing a rectangle directly. To do so one has to input all 4 points in the polygon function. 

For most purposes for saving bounding boxes ((x1,y1), width, height) is required. Example [cv2](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/23720875/how-to-draw-a-rectangle-around-a-region-of-interest-in-python).

While such a functionality is available in [matplotlib.patches.Rectangle](https://matplotlib.org/api/patches_api.html), it adds the rectangle to the figure and not the image.

issue_title: Remove minPctActiveDutyCycles and minActiveDutyCycles from Spatial Pooler
issue_body: The parameter `minPctActiveDutyCycles` is used by the old boosting rule. We can eliminate it now with the new boosting rule. The logic to compute and test `minActiveDutyCycles` can also be eliminated since it is only used for the old boosting calculation (as far as I can see).

issue_title: "Could not remove android presplash" if 'android' is not in requirements
issue_body:
If we use new toolchain we can see this message:

> [ERROR ] Base: Could not remove android presplash

It comes from [recent commit](https://github.com/kivy/kivy/commit/2d5b365747284b029c8a2b3291ca0f4d580e7b86) and happens if `android` is not in requirements.

I think this should be fixed, shouldn't it?

I see two possible ways:
- add `android` as mandatory dependency for new toolchain
- remove `remove_presplash` func [from](https://github.com/kivy/python-for-android/pull/930/commits/dbdc5158a7a077b589c5313b7ada135aa0f1084c) android module if it's possible

I don't know which one of ways is better.

GalSim's build-in methods to generate surface brightness profiles, for e.g. ```galsim.Gaussian```, ```galsim.OpticalPSF``` etc., can be explicitly convolved with the native pixel response and then drawn using a smaller pixel scale to give images that are finely sampled. However, when pixel level effects, like non-linearity and IPC have to be incorporated, then the images have to be drawn at the native pixel scale, which are typically sampled below Nyquist rate. Highly sampled images including these pixel level effects can be obtained from a dither sequence with uniform offsets by interleaving them, without having to invoke external image combination softwares.

issue_title: FA Pro - React Native - Invariant Violation Error
issue_body:
EDIT: this may be in the wrong repo 😬 

Hey!

I'm trying to get Font Awesome 5 Pro into a new app I'm making 🎉 

I've followed the documentation as best as I can, as there's no specific docs for React Native.

In my App.js - 
```javascript
import fontawesome from '@fortawesome/fontawesome'
import FontAwesomeIcon from '@fortawesome/react-fontawesome' // Maybe not needed?
import faCoffee from '@fortawesome/fontawesome-pro-light/faCoffee'

fontawesome.config =
  { familyPrefix: 'fal'
  }
fontawesome.library.add(faCoffee)
```

In a React component's `render` - 

```jsx
import FontAwesomeIcon from '@fortawesome/react-fontawesome'
<FontAwesomeIcon icon={route.icon} />
```

Where `route.icon` is currently `coffee`.

This, however, causes the following error to be thrown: 
```
Could not find icon, Object {
  "iconName": "coffee",
  "prefix": "fas",
}
- node_modules/@fortawesome/react-fontawesome/index.js:450:37 in log
```

Which is interesting, because according to the docs this is how I should be able to change the default prefix.

If I explicitly ask for that prefix using

```jsx
import FontAwesomeIcon from '@fortawesome/react-fontawesome'
<FontAwesomeIcon icon={[ 'fal', route.icon ]} />
```

This causes a _different_ error to be thrown:
```
Invariant Violation: View config not found for name path

This error is located at:
    in path (created by FontAwesomeIcon$1)
    in svg (created by FontAwesomeIcon$1)
    in FontAwesomeIcon$1 (at grid2.js:58)
    in RCTText (at Text.js:557)
    in Text (at rkText.js:82)
    in RkText (at grid2.js:55)
    in RCTView (at View.js:112)
```

This happens if I remove the config change as well, and also if I change the icon import from the `light` version to the `solid` one. Even importing the icon and using it directly in the component's `render` function doesn't work!

Any ideas?

issue_title: Problem using font-awesome within less - there is no icon mixin.

issue_body:
standard font-awesome.css has following declarations:

`.fa-camera-retro:before {  content: "\f083"; }`

which technically incorrect as `before`  is [pseudo-element but not pseudo-class](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Pseudo-elements). Therefore those declarations all should look like as

`.fa-camera-retro::before {  content: "\f083"; }`

While `:before` form is supported as an exception in most browsers I think for standard conformity that CSS should be changed.

issue_title: Replication slots don't prevent recycling of WAL when standby goes offline
issue_body: When standby goes offline for a while the member key will expire and Patroni will happily remove its replication slot.  Having an archive works around this, but it would be nice to make simple deployments more resilient too. Probably needs some kind of persistent flag, or offline_ttl for cluster members so Patroni knows they will be coming back and doesn't remove their replication slots. And a corresponding administration command to kick them out when they actually need to go away.

issue_title: Regression: pg_rewind is not called for Postgres v9.6.3
issue_body:
Commit https://github.com/zalando/patroni/commit/37c1552c0a834c7d40130551212a28ff9e286b04 breaks parsing of pg_controldata output for Postgres v9.6.3:
```
# LANG=C /usr/pgsql-9.6/bin/pg_controldata /var/lib/pgsql/9.6/data | grep -i wal_log_hints
wal_log_hints setting:                on
```
but https://github.com/zalando/patroni/blob/master/patroni/postgresql.py#L327:
```
    def configuration_allows_rewind(data):
        return data.get('Current wal_log_hints setting', 'off') == 'on' \
            or data.get('Data page checksum version', '0') != '0'
```
so this string never matches for recent Postgres versions.
There was fix for this before, but this commit erased it.

It would be nice if `patronictl` had a subcommand to list scheduled failovers.
The `list` subcommand could also have an additional column to indicate the closest scheduled failover (from and/or to member).

Hi,

Still playing with python-irodsclient on Windows, I found that calling `data_object_manager.open()` with `mode='w'` or `'w+'` uses bad flags on iRODS request.

At least `os.O_CREAT` doesn't have the same value on Windows than on Linux (which is the iCAT's OS in my case).
- Linux: `os.O_CREAT=64`
- Windows: `os.O_CREAT=256`

The catalog answers with an error for that reason. I checked than forcing python-irosdclient to use `64` for a flag on windows permits to have the good answer.

Is there a way to pass open requests in an OS independent way?

Thanks in advance,

Pierre

issue_title: Define and implement a versioning pattern on the web API
issue_body:
Currently we serve : 

- `api.openfisca.fr`, the "legacy" web API. 
- `fr.openfisca.org/api/v2`, the "preview" web API.

A breaking change in the france tax benefit system (e.g. variable renaming) or in the OpenFisca behaviour is _not_ reflected in the URL.

Ideally, a breaking change in the API should imply an URL change. 
We thus need to agree on a URL pattern.

## Open questions:
 - Should the `openfisca-france` major version be explicited in the URL ?
 - Should the `openfisca-core` major version be explicited in the URL ?
  
## Possibilities:
- `fr.openfisca.org/api/v5/parameters`, where `v5` implies a specific major version for france **and** for the API behaviour.
- `fr.openfisca.org/api/v5/parameters`, where `v5` implies a specific major version **just** for the API behaviour.
- `fr.openfisca.org/api/v2/fr-19/parameters`, where `fr-19` explicits the `france` version, and `v2` explicits the API behaviour.
- `fr.openfisca.org/api/of-18/fr-19`, where `fr-19` explicits the `france` version, and `of-18` explicits the core version.
- `fr.openfisca.org/api/v2/` with no guarantee at the moment (status quo)

## Possible strategies:
- Massive renaming in france to avoid later breaking changes
- Major release every _n_ months
- Meta-API to serve several api versions (costly)

## Deliverable:

The preview API is served on the chosen URL.

The current parameter syntax is very heavy, due to its format being XML. Its vocabulary is poorly readable and made mostly of French words and abbreviations. Some attributes are inconsistent. Example: ```xml # Suppression du bouclier fiscal à compter des revenus réalisés en 2011. # ``` This proposal aims at making it much easier to contribute to the parameters, by: 1. Improving their readability. 2. Decreasing the amount of purely syntactic tokens. 3. Unifying the vocabulary with the rest of OpenFisca (engine & API). 4. Improving diff readability. ## 1 - references There is currently no mechanism for storing value-level references. This is obviously making some critical information miss, and leads to dirty workarounds (see https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/issues/766) and lost contributors (see https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/pull/728#issuecomment-304023834). ### Proposition A ```yaml bouclier_fiscal.taux: description: Bouclier fiscal type: percentage values: 2006-01-01: 0.6 2007-01-01: 0.5 2011-01-01: null references: 2006-01-01: http://example.org 2007-01-01: 2011-01-01: http://example.org ``` ### Proposition B ```yaml bouclier_fiscal.taux: description: Bouclier fiscal type: percentage values: 2006-01-01: value: 0.6 reference: http://example.org 2007-01-01: value: 0.5 2011-01-01: value: null reference: http://example.org ``` ## 2 - names _(assuming format 1B for references)_ ### Proposition A Inline hierarchy in the ID: ```yaml bouclier_fiscal.taux: description: Bouclier fiscal type: percentage values: 2006-01-01: value: 0.6 reference: http://example.org 2007-01-01: value: 0.5 2011-01-01: value: null reference: http://example.org ``` ### Proposition B Keep the current hierarchy: ```yaml bouclier_fiscal: taux: description: Bouclier fiscal type: percentage values: 2006-01-01: value: 0.6 reference: http://example.org 2007-01-01: value: 0.5 2011-01-01: value: null reference: http://example.org ``` Means there would be a few reserved names in order to identify leaves (such as `description`, `type`, `values`). ### Proposition C Rely on filesystem hierarchy: ```yaml # bouclier_fiscal.yaml taux: description: Bouclier fiscal type: percentage values: 2006-01-01: value: 0.6 reference: http://example.org 2007-01-01: value: 0.5 2011-01-01: value: null reference: http://example.org ``` ## Open questions 1. What are the [`conflicts` attributes](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/pull/749#discussion_r118240100) used for? Can they be dropped? If not, how to represent them? 2. Can the `origin` attributes be absorbed in the `reference` attributes? I.e. can an opaque identifier be systematically superseded by an URI? (I frame it this way to show that it is definitely technically feasible, and that the question is purely semantic) 3. What is the exact correspondence table between the current `type` + `format` attributes couple and the new `type` attribute? Is it bijective? 4. Can this new format be thought of without taking into account table parameters (https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-core/issues/473)? If not, how can we handle them?

\\u00a0\\u1680\\u2000-\\u200a\\u2028\\u2029\\u202f\\u205f\\u30000-9\\u00b2\\u00b3\\u00b9\\u00bc-\\u00be\\u0660-\\u0669\\u06f0-\\u06f9\\u07c0-\\u07c9\\u0966-\\u096f\\u09e6-\\u09ef\\u09f4-\\u09f9\\u0a66-\\u0a6f\\u0ae6-\\u0aef\\u0b66-\\u0b6f\\u0b72-\\u0b77\\u0be6-\\u0bf2\\u0c66-\\u0c6f\\u0c78-\\u0c7e\\u0ce6-\\u0cef\\u0d66-\\u0d75\\u0de6-\\u0def\\u0e50-\\u0e59\\u0ed0-\\u0ed9\\u0f20-\\u0f33\\u1040-\\u1049\\u1090-\\u1099\\u1369-\\u137c\\u16ee-\\u16f0\\u17e0-\\u17e9\\u17f0-\\u17f9\\u1810-\\u1819\\u1946-\\u194f\\u19d0-\\u19da\\u1a80-\\u1a89\\u1a90-\\u1a99\\u1b50-\\u1b59\\u1bb0-\\u1bb9\\u1c40-\\u1c49\\u1c50-\\u1c59\\u2070\\u2074-\\u2079\\u2080-\\u2089\\u2150-\\u2182\\u2185-\\u2189\\u2460-\\u249b\\u24ea-\\u24ff\\u2776-\\u2793\\u2cfd\\u3007\\u3021-\\u3029\\u3038-\\u303a\\u3192-\\u3195\\u3220-\\u3229\\u3248-\\u324f\\u3251-\\u325f\\u3280-\\u3289\\u32b1-\\u32bf\\ua620-\\ua629\\ua6e6-\\ua6ef\\ua830-\\ua835\\ua8d0-\\ua8d9\\ua900-\\ua909\\ua9d0-\\ua9d9\\ua9f0-\\ua9f9\\uaa50-\\uaa59\\uabf0-\\uabf9\\uff10-\\uff19_.:\/%&#=+\\-@]{1,200}$",
      "java_pattern": "([\\p{L}\\p{Z}\\p{N}_.:/%&#=+\\-@]*)$"
    },
```

However, the SDK is dropping characters that are not ASCII: https://github.com/aws/aws-xray-sdk-python/blob/master/aws_xray_sdk/core/models/entity.py#L18.

If a segment/subsegment name has invalid characters, that segment/subsegment will not be accepted by X-Ray service back-end. But since the X-Ray daemon sends segments on batches, the invalid segment/subsegment will be in "Unprocessed" of the API `PutTraceSegments` but the call will still succeeds. That is the background of the sanitization happening on the SDK side to make sure valuable data will not be dropped due to invalid characters on names.

The SDK should have equal or at least loosen restriction than the back-end has. Doing a full regex using `([\\p{L}\\p{Z}\\p{N}_.:/%&#=+\\-@]*)$` adds performance overhead since this regex match happens for each single segment/subsegment capture.

The purpose is to have the SDK to switch to blacklist based sanitization. It drops common invalid characters like `? * $ ; ( ) [ ] { }`. This ensures the lightweight design and unicode letters from non-English nature languages pass through.

Any feedback is welcome.

issue_title: Implement executor shutdown mechanism with an event
issue_body:
Currently executor shutdown is implemented as a conditional variable. Which is difficult to maintain.
multiprocessing.Event is better suited

        self._shutdown = manager.Value('i', 0)

This is just from a quick look over unit test coverage report results. What actually needs coverage may be another story.

The following have no coverage in `tests/ETH/*`;
- STOP
- CALLDATACOPY
- CODECOPY
- EXTCODECOPY
- RETURNDATACOPY
- RETURNDATASIZE
- BLOCKHASH
- MLOAD
- MSTORE
- MSTORE8 (tainted pc)
- SSTORE (tainted pc)
- JUMPI
- SWAP
- LOG
- CREATE
- CALL
- CALLCODE
- RETURN
- DELEGATECALL
- STATICCALL
- THROW


...

I tried to use huber_distance to calculate the distance between two input vectors but the result is not quite as expected.
```
import dynet as dy

ex = dy.vecInput(1)
ex.set([0])
ey = dy.vecInput(1)
ey.set([5])

e = dy.huber_distance(ex, ey, c=1.345)
print(e.value())
```

The output result is 11.6409749985.

Actually, the right way t calculate, according to the [API: huber loss](http://dynet.readthedocs.io/en/latest/operations.html?highlight=huber#_CPPv3N5dynet14huber_distanceERK10ExpressionRK10Expressionf), Because 5-0=5>c=1.345, the output is 1.345*5-0.5*1.345^2=5.8204875 but somehow, the actually is 2 times the desired output.

In another case:
```
import dynet as dy

ex = dy.vecInput(1)
ex.set([0])
ey = dy.vecInput(1)
ey.set([1])

e = dy.huber_distance(ex, ey, c=1.345)
print(e.value())
```

The output result is 1.0.

Actually the desired output should be 0.5*(1-0)^2 = 0.5.

Also, in the [API: huber loss](http://dynet.readthedocs.io/en/latest/operations.html?highlight=huber#_CPPv3N5dynet14huber_distanceERK10ExpressionRK10Expressionf), the `lr` is somehow confusing when `|y-f(x)|<=c`.

Albeit Dynet is primarly intended for NLP applications, I think it would gain traction if it supported vision specific operations such as 2d conv and pooling (which, to my knowledge, it doesn't).

I have no experience with efficiently implementing these ops in cuda/eigen so if anyone knows/can provide pointers I'd be glad to help.

issue_title: Remove / from the beginning of the '/reactivate' url
issue_body: Django system checks is complaining about it.

I've been scoping out the testing-cabal packages to make some [conda](http://conda.pydata.org/docs/) builds for [conda-forge](https://conda-forge.github.io/). In doing so, I've found what seems to be a circular dependency: `testtools` needs `fixtures >=1.3.0` which itself needs `testtools >=0.9.22`

line 159 of healtcheck/__init__.py in dump_environment there is a call to self.functions.iteritems():

Python 3 does not support iteritems on dicts, which breaks the ability to actually do an environment dump if using Python 3

Drücken Sie eine beliebige Taste . . .
```

Attempting to build [Source Code Regular](https://github.com/adobe-fonts/source-code-pro/tree/master/Roman/Instances/Regular) will fail with the following message,
```
syntax error at "}" missing ";" [font.ufo/../../../../familyTables.fea 3]
```

There's clearly a semicolon at https://github.com/adobe-fonts/source-code-pro/blob/master/familyTables.fea#L2

I can get around this issue by adding a semicolon to https://github.com/adobe-fonts/source-code-pro/blob/master/Roman/Instances/familyVersion.fea#L2 but that shouldn't be required, I think.

issue_title: TODO: Change Unit() to support compound units
issue_body:
It is often the case that a user may want to work in terms of concentration say µg/mL. Currently `Unit()` does not support dimensional division nor multiplication.

Some ideas have been batted around to integrate Pint https://pint.readthedocs.org/en/0.6/ into the Autoprotocol-python library to build out the `Unit()` class with this support. It would be ideal to not introduce breaking changes.

It would be great to have some discussion about this here.

1.  First, upgrade all packages from `0.20dev` to `0.20.0` and solidify bounds for dependencies.

    ```
    mkdir ${HOME}/tmp-release
    cd ${HOME}/tmp-release
    git clone https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python

1.  Then go into each package and do a release

    ```
    # core
    cd ${HOME}/tmp-release/google-cloud-python/core

    twine upload dist/*
    # Similar for the 14 packages:

    # logging, monitoring, pubsub, resource_manager, speech,
    # storage, translate, vision

    ```
    cd ${HOME}/tmp-release/google-cloud-python
    python setup.py sdist
    twine upload dist/*    
    ```

Service running on-premises

issue_title: investigate cold runtime performance post-native engine
issue_body:
post-native engine, a v2 list in a large repo is still ~6.5x slower than cold:

```
[illuminati source (pants/1.3.0dev2)]$ time ./pants list ::
...

real    1m27.712s
user    0m58.901s
sys     0m19.068s
```

```
[illuminati source (pants/1.3.0dev2)]$ time ./pants --enable-v2-engine list ::
...

real    9m32.394s
user    8m22.233s
sys     0m33.890s
```

this is much better than our previous 11x slowdown, but our fundamental goal was closer to ~2x v1.

we'll want to dig into this to understand the optimization path to get to <=2x v1.

issue_title: --print-exception-stacktrace has no effect on python exceptions from within Rules

If BUILD file parsing or a Rule implementation throw an exception, we only get the exception message.

issue_title: Scout2 does not detect credentials when running on Amazon ECS
issue_body:
When running Scout2 on ECS it appears to not be able to retrieve the instance credentials provided by the IAM role for the task.

When running Scout2 with no arguments it returns:
Error: could not find AWS credentials. Use the --help option for more information.

I suspect that this is due to an updated metadata URL specific to container instances. 

EC2 metadata:
http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/

ECS metadata:
curl 169.254.170.2$AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_RELATIVE_URI
where an example of  $AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_RELATIVE_URI=/v2/credentials/9f426f92-8cb7-49fe-9353-3284d427a46f

More information about IAM roles with ECS tasks can be found here:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/task-iam-roles.html

issue_title: Add support for object level tagging
issue_body: Blocking MISP issue: https://github.com/MISP/MISP/issues/2638

in MarathonTask
```python
def __init__(
        self, app_id=None, health_check_results=None, host=None, id=None, ports=None, service_ports=None,
                 slave_id=None, staged_at=None, started_at=None, version=None, ip_addresses=[]):
        self.app_id = app_id
        self.health_check_results = health_check_results or []
        self.health_check_results = [
            hcr if isinstance(
                hcr, MarathonHealthCheckResult) else MarathonHealthCheckResult().from_json(hcr)
            for hcr in (health_check_results or []) if any(health_check_results)
        ]
        self.host = host
        self.id = id
        self.ports = ports or []
        self.service_ports = service_ports or []
        self.slave_id = slave_id
        self.staged_at = staged_at if (staged_at is None or isinstance(staged_at, datetime)) \
            else datetime.strptime(staged_at, self.DATETIME_FORMAT)
        self.started_at = started_at if (started_at is None or isinstance(started_at, datetime)) \
            else datetime.strptime(started_at, self.DATETIME_FORMAT)
        self.version = version
```

ip_addresses are passed but not set to self.ip_addresses

Function **_example_from_array_spec** in **swagger_parser.py** contains the following line:
`return example_dict[example_dict.keys()[0]]`
which throws 

> TypeError: 'dict_keys' object does not support indexing

 error in Python 3 since `example_dict.keys()` returns a _view object_ not a list.
It should be something like `example_dict[list(example_dict)[0]]` in order to work for Python 3.

issue_title: How to use a dockerfile not named "Dockerfile" in `images.build(path=..)`?
issue_body:
This works well when my dockerfile is named `Dockerfile` in that path:
```
import os
import inspect
path_to_here = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(inspect.getfile(inspect.currentframe())))
client.images.build(
    path=path_to_here,
    tag="myimage"
)
```

Is there a way to make it work and keep it as simple when my dockerfile is named something else, e.g. `Dockerfile.test`?

Hi :)

I would like to subprocess any given command and log its stdout and stderr separately. This would seem like an easy thing to do, but im having no luck because:
1) Processes which detect that their stdout/stderr are not ttys will modify their output
2) Processes which detect that their stdout/stderr are not the same file path will assume theres stdout redirection and modify their output.

So in jumps ptys and ptyprocess to the resuce. Tie the stdout and stderr to a pty, you get 1 file path like  /dev/tty0021, and isatty() returns true for both. Problem is - now we can't distinguish stdout from stderr.
Ok, no problem, just make two ptys - one for stdout and one for stderr. But now although both pass isatty(), their file paths will now look like /dev/tty0021 and /dev/tty0022 (for example).
The subprocess reacts as if you weren't using a pty in the first place, and you log nothing.

I have been trying for four months to figure a way around this, and because you are the expert in ptys I thought i might ask you directly - could you think of a way to log stdout and stderr separatly in your program, and still fool a script like this: https://bpaste.net/show/000d6f70ef41

THANK YOU :D

issue_title: Aria2 fails as an external downloader on Windows
issue_body:
### Subject of the issue
Aria2 (and presumably other external downloaders) fails as an external downloader on Windows

### Your environment
* Operating System (name/version):  Win7
* Python version: 3.4
* coursera-dl version: 0.6.1

### Steps to reproduce
Use the "--aria" option on Windows.


* Is the problem happening with the latest version of the script?

Yes.

* Do you have all the recommended versions of the modules? See them in the
  file `requirements.txt`.

Yes.

* What is the course that you are trying to access?

Any.

* What is the precise command line that you are using (don't forget to obfuscate
  your username and password, but leave all other information untouched).

Any "--aria" option on Windows.


* What are the precise messages that you get? Please, use the `--debug`
  option before posting the messages as a bug report. Please, copy and paste
  them.  Don't reword/paraphrase the messages.

The introduction of the "\\\?\" path prefix to deal with long pathnames on Windows breaks Aria2, and possibly the other external downloaders, which can't deal with pathnames with that format.

I have a fix for this already, but I'm waiting for #533 to be addressed, so I can run the tests before making a pull request.


### Expected behaviour

External downloaders should work.


### Actual behaviour

External downloaders fail, with a failure to create the target file.

issue_title: Creating empty environment can trick user
issue_body:
When creating an empty environment, as suggested by the documentation, a real gotcha is that installing by pip will install into the root environment. I suggest that the documentation be updated to warn the user. 

To clarify - when a user creates a new **empty** environment, pip is not installed into this environment, but is still linked from root. I can foresee new users installing conda, creating a new environment, and then installing their favourite package via pip, and consequently running into issues when the package cannot be found.

Previously, this was not an issue, since environments were created by `conda create --name myenv PACKAGE`, which wasn't very intuitive, but prevented an empty environment from being created.

issue_title: recurring error when trying to work with/install packages for R "sh: symbol lookup error: sh: undefined symbol: rl_signal_event_hook"
issue_body:
This has eaten my whole day and I finally gave up and resolved to bother you guys.

A few things up front:
- I run Arch Linux which seems to cause similar readline issue to those faced by mac folks when dealing with IPython etc (but NOT outside of the conda environments -- its kinda of reversed)
- I fixed the problems by building and installing a gnureadline pkg myself (http://xguse.github.io/conda-package-repo/pkgs/channel/linux-64/gnureadline-6.2.5-py27_0.tar.bz2)
- the things I am failing to install are bioconductor packages (kill me now)

I mention this because my issue seems to intersect with readline issues.  

I can install the conda packages from the r channel fine.  But when I try to adapt the recipes to build bioconductor packages they always choke.

I then just tried to run my conda R install and do the

```
source("http://bioconductor.org/biocLite.R")
biocLite()
```

process that bioconductor claims is the only SANE way to install their stuff.

I am met with `sh: symbol lookup error: sh: undefined symbol: rl_signal_event_hook`. It is driving me nuts.

My question is twofold:
1. insight into what is going on would be appreciated.
2. can you PLEASE include text in the conda docs on installing things from bioconductor and provide a few more educational examples of recipes for that kind of thing?

Thanks.

Gus

issue_title: Installing package from conda-forge also updgrades conda and other unrelated packages from conda-forge
issue_body:
## Current Behavior I intended to install Miktex from the conda-forge channel using conda version 4.5.5 and this caused conda to be updated to the version on conda-forge, and several other packages (many of which I think are not Miktex dependencies) were installed, updated, or downgraded to versions on conda-forge. ### Steps to Reproduce My python distribution is from Anaconda. I had previously only installed packages with conda from the default channels and through pip. My condarc file was empty. I installed Miktex from conda-forge using `conda install -c conda-forge Miktex` ## Expected Behavior I intended to only install Miktex and dependencies that I didn't have yet. issue_title: hardclipped bases ignored with The pysam.AlignedSegment.infer_query_length
issue_body:
The [ `pysam.AlignedSegment.infer_query_length`](http://pysam.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#pysam.AlignedSegment.infer_query_length) method ignores hardclipped bases.  The method states that it gets the:

> inferred read length from CIGAR string.

this is in contrast with the [`pysam.AlignedSegment.query_length `](http://pysam.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#pysam.AlignedSegment.query_length)

>This value corresponds to the length of the sequence supplied in the BAM/SAM file. The length of a query is 0 if there is no sequence in the BAM/SAM file. In those cases, the read length can be inferred  from the CIGAR alignment, see `pysam.AlignmentFile.infer_query_length()`.

With a cigar value of `58H150M` which represents an alignment where 58 bases have been hardclipped (not present in the alignment or SEQ section) and 150 matches. The SEQ section has 150 bases.  If `query_length=150` and `infer_query_length()=150`, however this should be: `infer_query_length()=208` by my understanding of the docs.  Am I missing something / is this intentional or just something to be corrected at some point?

Example of this can be found and run here: https://gist.github.com/amblina/795d30b5e04f87090d61739cb1846c1b 

Output:
```
@HD	VN:1.3	SO:unsorted
@SQ	SN:Test_reference	LN:200
query1	0	Test_reference	51	255	58H150M	*	0	0	TCTCCGACTGTTCCCAAATTGTAACTTATCGTTCCGTGAAGGCCAGAGTCACTTCCCGGCCCTTTCCATGCGCGCACCATACCCTCCTAGTTCCCCGGTTATCTTTCCGAAGTGGGAGTGAGCGAACCTCCGTTTACGTCTTGTTACCAA	*	NM:i:1	AS:i:148

Infered length of query: 150
SEQ field length: 150
CIGAR field: 58H150M, length: 208
Actual length of original query: 208
```

It seems to me that the way data is chunked for the bulk api could be wrong.  Specifically, it could happen that the elastic bulk api receives incomplete/malformed json request when the data is chunked outside document boundaries.

... or am I missing something?

issue_title: Unable to install az from Ubuntu 18.04 bionic
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
Unable to install a Microsoft signing key for `az` which works on Ubuntu 18.04 bionic and consequentially unable to install `az` via `apt`.

**To Reproduce**
Follow instructions in the document: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/install-azure-cli-apt?view=azure-cli-latest 
```
sudo apt-key adv --keyserver packages.microsoft.com --recv-keys 52E16F86FEE04B979B07E28DB02C46DF417A0893
Executing: /tmp/apt-key-gpghome.8anL89cvRR/gpg.1.sh --keyserver packages.microsoft.com --recv-keys 52E16F86FEE04B979B07E28DB02C46DF417A0893
gpg: connecting dirmngr at '/tmp/apt-key-gpghome.8anL89cvRR/S.dirmngr' failed: IPC connect call failed
gpg: keyserver receive failed: No dirmngr
```
The new signing key installs properly (`curl -L https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc | sudo apt-key add -`, but this key still doesn't allow me to download from the `apt` source.
```
sudo apt-key list
...
pub   rsa2048 2015-10-28 [SC]
      BC52 8686 B50D 79E3 39D3  721C EB3E 94AD BE12 29CF
uid           [ unknown] Microsoft (Release signing) <gpgsecurity@microsoft.com>
```
Then this is the error when doing the `sudo apt-get update`
```
Err:4 https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/azure-cli bionic InRelease
  The following signatures couldn't be verified because the public key is not available: NO_PUBKEY B02C46DF417A0893
Reading package lists... Done
W: GPG error: https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/azure-cli bionic InRelease: The following signatures couldn't be verified because the public key is not available: NO_PUBKEY B02C46DF417A0893

N: Updating from such a repository can't be done securely, and is therefore disabled by default.
N: See apt-secure(8) manpage for repository creation and user configuration details.
```

**Expected behavior**
Either the old or new signing key allows me to `sudo apt-get update` from the `az` apt source.

**Environment summary**
Bash on Windows with 18.04 (bionic) LTS

**Additional context**
`lsb-release` and `dirmngr` are installed and the latest versions.

it needs to be changed to 2016-01-01

### Description
I can't seem to get the right incantation to set the WrapKey or UnwrapKey permissions on keys in Azure Key Vault using the Azure CLI;

```
az keyvault set-policy --name $keyvault --object-id $id--key-permissions get list wrap_key unwrap_key

Fails with:

usage: az keyvault set-policy [-h] [--output {json,tsv,table,jsonc}]

From reading the docs for the [management library](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/python/api/azure.mgmt.keyvault.models.keypermissions?view=azure-python), `wrap_key` and `unwrap_key` should be the right names, but no idea what is the right mechanism to do it.

result = resource_client.resource_groups.create_or_update(
    GROUP_NAME,
    azure.mgmt.resource.resources.models.ResourceGroup(
        location=REGION,
    ),
)

# Create Storage Group
result = storage_client.storage_accounts.create(
    GROUP_NAME,
    STORAGE_NAME,
    azure.mgmt.storage.models.StorageAccountCreateParameters(
        location=REGION,
        account_type=azure.mgmt.storage.models.AccountType.standard_lrs,
    ),
)
result.wait()



def create_network_interface(network_client, region, group_name, interface_name, network_name, subnet_name, ip_name):
    result = network_client.virtual_networks.create_or_update(
        group_name,
        network_name,
        azure.mgmt.network.models.VirtualNetwork(
            location=region,
            address_space=azure.mgmt.network.models.AddressSpace(
                address_prefixes=[
                    '10.1.0.0/16',
                ],
            ),
            subnets=[
                azure.mgmt.network.models.Subnet(
                    name=subnet_name,
                    address_prefix='10.1.0.0/24',
                ),
            ],
        ),
    )
    result.wait()
    subnet = network_client.subnets.get(group_name, network_name, subnet_name)
    result = network_client.public_ip_addresses.create_or_update(
        group_name,
        ip_name,
        azure.mgmt.network.models.PublicIPAddress(
            location=region,
            public_ip_allocation_method=azure.mgmt.network.models.IPAllocationMethod.dynamic,
            idle_timeout_in_minutes=4,
        ),
    )
    result.wait()
    public_ip_address = network_client.public_ip_addresses.get(group_name, ip_name)
    public_ip_id = public_ip_address.id
    result = network_client.network_interfaces.create_or_update(
        group_name,
        interface_name,
        azure.mgmt.network.models.NetworkInterface(
            name=interface_name,
            location=region,
            ip_configurations=[
                azure.mgmt.network.models.NetworkInterfaceIPConfiguration(
                    name='default',
                    private_ip_allocation_method=azure.mgmt.network.models.IPAllocationMethod.dynamic,
                    subnet=subnet,
                    public_ip_address=azure.mgmt.network.models.PublicIPAddress(
                        id=public_ip_id,
                    ),
                ),
            ],
        ),
    )
    time.sleep(30)  # result.wait() throws errors, but need to wait before getting the ID
    network_interface = network_client.network_interfaces.get(
        group_name,
        interface_name,
    )
    return network_interface.id


nic_id = create_network_interface(
    network_client,
    REGION,
    GROUP_NAME,
    NETWORK_INTERFACE_NAME,
    VIRTUAL_NETWORK_NAME,
    SUBNET_NAME,
    PUBLIC_IP_NAME,
)

result = compute_client.virtual_machines.create_or_update(
    GROUP_NAME,
    VM_NAME,
    azure.mgmt.compute.models.VirtualMachine(
        location=REGION,
        name=VM_NAME,
        os_profile=azure.mgmt.compute.models.OSProfile(
            admin_username=ADMIN_USERNAME,
            admin_password=ADMIN_PASSWORD,
            computer_name=COMPUTER_NAME,
        ),
        hardware_profile=azure.mgmt.compute.models.HardwareProfile(
          vm_size="Basic_A0"
        ),
        network_profile=azure.mgmt.compute.models.NetworkProfile(

                    id=nic_id,
                ),
            ],
        ),
        storage_profile=azure.mgmt.compute.models.StorageProfile(
            os_disk=azure.mgmt.compute.models.OSDisk(
                caching=azure.mgmt.compute.models.CachingTypes.none,
                create_option=azure.mgmt.compute.models.DiskCreateOptionTypes.from_image,

                ),
            )

)
try:

finally:

issue_title: Uploaded temp files should be 256mb in size
issue_body:
The chunking process needs to be two tiered:
1. At most 4mb of data is uploaded in a single http request
2. The intermediate files should be as close to 256mb as possible.

This is important for a couple of reasons:
1. 4mb upload chunk size is the only way to guarantee atomicity of the upload operation for a single request. For example, if you upload 16mb in a single request and it fails, on retry you need to see if 0, 4, 8 or 12mb of data were uploaded (since the server will always commit in 4mb chunks).
2. The intermediate files should be as closed to 256mb as possible to avoid massive fragmentation and lifecycle performance problems once the data is in the system. 256mb is the optimal size in our system for chunks to be concatenated at.

So, for example, if you have a 10GB file it should be split into ~40 chunks of 256mb. Each of those chunks can be uploaded in parallel using <= 4mb requests.
